probe: int4 QAT W4A16 greedy run-to-run reconciliation: prefix-cache OFF (diagnostic only)
purpose: Test advisor 11:47 prediction + my own 11:45 suggested follow-up: re-run cudagraph M=1 greedy back-to-back with --no-enable-prefix-caching using a MATCHED int4-Marlin reference (same arithmetic as the endpoint). Isolate prefix-cache/scheduler state as the run-to-run driver and reconcile PR#3 DIVERGENT with the within-stack IDENTICAL controls.
one_variable_delta: Identical to the prior determinism_probe 'cudagraph M=1 run1 vs run2' (the REAL submission config, which was 2/8 DIVERGENT) EXCEPT enable_prefix_caching flipped true -> false. Nothing else changed.

comparisons.name: within-process run1 vs run2
comparisons.regime: same server process A, M=1 sequential, prefix-cache OFF

comparisons.name: cross-process run1 vs run3
comparisons.regime: run1 from process A vs run3 from a freshly restarted process B, identical config, M=1 sequential, prefix-cache OFF

original_PR3_HF_bf16_dense_AR_reference_.note: cross-PATH reference (bf16-dense GEMM) vs int4-Marlin endpoint -> near-tie divergence regardless of reproducibility

conclusions: Turning prefix caching OFF flips cudagraph M=1 run-to-run from 2/8 DIVERGENT to 8/8 byte-identical (0 divergent tokens). One-variable result: the run-to-run noise was prefix-cache / scheduler state, NOT the Marlin int4 GEMM or Triton-attn kernel.

int4-Marlin M=1 greedy is bit-exact run-to-run BOTH within-process AND cross-process when prefix caching is off and the reference uses the same int4-Marlin arithmetic. This confirms the advisor 11:47 prediction and matches denken's relayed cross-process bit-exact regime, reproduced independently on the stark stack.

Reconciles my PR#3 DIVERGENT finding with the within-stack IDENTICAL controls: my original divergence was driven by (a) a cross-PATH bf16-dense AR reference vs the int4-Marlin endpoint, and (b) prefix-cache state across separate warm runs. Remove both (matched int4-Marlin reference + prefix-cache OFF) and the stark stack reproduces bit-exact. The int4 base CAN self-certify the greedy gate in the gate-relevant regime (M=1 sequential, prefix-cache OFF, matched arithmetic).

Prefix caching with chunked prefill reuses cached KV blocks whose values differ by ~1 ULP from a fresh full recompute and shifts chunk boundaries; at a near-tie logit this flips one argmax and the sequence cascades. Disabling prefix cache forces an identical fresh computation every run -> reproducible.
submission_impact: NONE. The shipped submission (submissions/int4_qat/serve.py) deliberately keeps prefix caching ON (vLLM default) for serving throughput; this probe does NOT recommend shipping --no-enable-prefix-caching. The prefix-cache-OFF setting is purely the diagnostic regime that isolates the mechanism and matches an AR-reference comparison. No submission file changed; no HF job launched; this is not the deferred VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT determinism-enabling work.
